paper_title: Tent: Fully Test-time Adaptation by Entropy Minimization
paper_abstract:
A model must adapt itself to generalize to new and different data during
testing. In this setting of fully test-time adaptation the model has only the
test data and its own parameters. We propose to adapt by test entropy
minimization (tent): we optimize the model for confidence as measured by the
entropy of its predictions. Our method estimates normalization statistics and
optimizes channel-wise affine transformations to update online on each batch.
Tent reduces generalization error for image classification on corrupted
ImageNet and CIFAR-10/100 and reaches a new state-of-the-art error on
ImageNet-C. Tent handles source-free domain adaptation on digit recognition
from SVHN to MNIST/MNIST-M/USPS, on semantic segmentation from GTA to
Cityscapes, and on the VisDA-C benchmark. These results are achieved in one
epoch of test-time optimization without altering training.

findings: Fully Test-Time Adaptation Setting: An adaptation paradigm in which a pretrained model f_{θ} is deployed on test (target) data x^t without any access to the original training (source) data or labels.  During inference, the model may update itself using only the unlabeled target data.  Unlike fine-tuning (which requires target labels), domain adaptation (which requires source and target data), or test-time training (which requires a modified training phase with self-supervision on source), fully test-time adaptation relies solely on the test loss L(x^t) and target inputs x^t.

Entropy–Error Correlation under Dataset Shift: An empirical observation that, on corrupted datasets such as CIFAR-100-C, predictions with lower Shannon entropy are statistically more likely to be correct.  This rank correlation between entropy and classification error motivates entropy minimization as a proxy objective for reducing test error under distribution shift.

State-of-the-Art Robustness on ImageNet-C: By applying Tent online (one gradient step per batch) on ImageNet-C, the method achieves 44.0% top-1 error—surpassing prior robust-training methods such as Adversarial Noise Training (50.2%) and AugMix (51.7%)—and 42.3% error in offline mode.  This demonstrates that fully test-time adaptation alone, without altering source training, can set new benchmarks in corruption robustness.

Efficient Adaptation via Low-Dimensional Updates: Tent requires updating fewer than 1% of the model parameters (only the normalization affine γ,β) and performs just one backward gradient per test batch.  In digit adaptation tasks, this translates to around 80× less computation than conventional domain-adaptation methods (which train on both source and target for multiple epochs) while still achieving competitive or superior error rates.

Generalization Across Architectures: Tent's test-time entropy minimization and feature-modulation framework extends beyond residual networks.  When applied without hyperparameter tuning to self-attention networks (SAN-10) and multiscale deep equilibrium models (MDEQ), Tent consistently reduces error on CIFAR-100-C, confirming the method's architectural agnosticism.

code_overview:
---

## Summary
Tent is a lightweight, efficient solution for test-time adaptation, particularly suited for handling distribution shifts in real-world scenarios. Its modular design and clear examples make it accessible for both research and practical applications. The project highlights the effectiveness of entropy minimization for improving model robustness during inference.

techniques.name: Tent (Test Entropy Minimization)
techniques.description: A fully test-time adaptation procedure that adapts a pretrained, probabilistic, differentiable model during inference by minimizing the entropy of its predictions on incoming target data.  Tent treats the batch of test examples as an unlabeled dataset and, for each batch, estimates normalization statistics, computes prediction entropy, and back-propagates the entropy loss to update a low-dimensional set of feature modulation parameters.  This yields a test-time-adapted model that consistently reduces both prediction entropy and classification error under dataset shift.

code.implementation:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
import torch.optim as optim
from torch import Tensor
from typing import List, Tuple, Literal, Optional
from copy import deepcopy


def softmax_entropy(logits: Tensor) -> Tensor:
    """
    Compute the Shannon entropy for each sample in a batch from model logits.

    Given logits z of shape (batch_size, num_classes), this function
    applies softmax to obtain class probabilities and then computes
    entropy H(p) = -sum_c p_c * log(p_c) for each sample.

    Args:
        logits (Tensor): Unnormalized model outputs of shape (B, C).

    Returns:
        Tensor: Shape (B,) containing the entropy of each sample's predicted distribution.
    """
    log_probs = F.log_softmax(logits, dim=1)
    probs = torch.exp(log_probs)
    entropy = -(probs * log_probs).sum(dim=1)
    return entropy


def entropy_minimization_loss(
    logits: Tensor,
    reduction: Literal['mean', 'sum', 'none'] = 'mean'
) -> Tensor:
    """
    Compute the entropy minimization loss over a batch of logits.

    This unsupervised loss is defined as the Shannon entropy of the
    model's output distribution. Minimizing this encourages the model
    to produce confident (low-entropy) predictions.

    Args:
        logits (Tensor): Unnormalized model outputs of shape (B, C).
        reduction (str): One of 'mean', 'sum', or 'none'.
            'mean'   – average entropy over the batch.
            'sum'    – sum of entropies over the batch.
            'none'   – per-sample entropy (returns shape (B,)).

    Returns:
        Tensor: Scalar if reduction is 'mean' or 'sum', else shape (B,).
    """
    ent = softmax_entropy(logits)
    if reduction == 'mean':
        return ent.mean()
    elif reduction == 'sum':
        return ent.sum()
    elif reduction == 'none':
        return ent
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid reduction mode: {reduction}")


def copy_model_and_optimizer(
    model: nn.Module,
    optimizer: optim.Optimizer
) -> Tuple[dict, dict]:
    """
    Deep-copy model and optimizer states for episodic reset.

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): The model to copy.
        optimizer (Optimizer): The optimizer to copy.

    Returns:
        Tuple[dict, dict]: (model_state_dict, optimizer_state_dict)
    """
    model_state = deepcopy(model.state_dict())
    optim_state = deepcopy(optimizer.state_dict())
    return model_state, optim_state


def load_model_and_optimizer(
    model: nn.Module,
    optimizer: optim.Optimizer,
    model_state: dict,
    optim_state: dict
) -> None:
    """
    Restore model and optimizer states from copies.

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): Model to restore.
        optimizer (Optimizer): Optimizer to restore.
        model_state (dict): A state_dict for the model.
        optim_state (dict): A state_dict for the optimizer.
    """
    model.load_state_dict(model_state)
    optimizer.load_state_dict(optim_state)


def collect_bn_affine_params(
    model: nn.Module
) -> Tuple[List[nn.Parameter], List[str]]:
    """
    Collect the affine scale (weight) and shift (bias) parameters
    from all BatchNorm2d layers in the model.

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): The model containing BatchNorm2d layers.

    Returns:
        params (List[nn.Parameter]): List of parameters to be adapted.
        names  (List[str]): List of parameter names corresponding to `params`.
    """
    params: List[nn.Parameter] = []
    names: List[str] = []
    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if isinstance(module, nn.BatchNorm2d):
            for param_name, param in module.named_parameters(recurse=False):
                if param_name in ("weight", "bias"):
                    params.append(param)
                    names.append(f"{module_name}.{param_name}")
    return params, names


def configure_model_for_bn_affine(model: nn.Module) -> nn.Module:
    """
    Configure a neural network model for test-time adaptation via
    BatchNorm affine parameters only.

    - Puts the model in train mode so that BatchNorm uses batch statistics.
    - Disables gradients for all parameters, then re-enables gradients only
      for BatchNorm2d weight and bias.
    - Forces BatchNorm2d layers to use per-batch statistics (no running average).

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): The pre-trained model to configure.

    Returns:
        nn.Module: The same model, configured for adaptation.
    """
    model.train()
    model.requires_grad_(False)

    for module in model.modules():
        if isinstance(module, nn.BatchNorm2d):
            # enable gradient only for affine parameters
            module.requires_grad_(True)
            # force per-batch normalization (no running stats)
            module.track_running_stats = False
            module.running_mean = None  # type: ignore
            module.running_var = None   # type: ignore

    return model


@torch.enable_grad()
def forward_and_adapt(
    x: Tensor,
    model: nn.Module,
    optimizer: optim.Optimizer
) -> Tensor:
    """
    Perform a forward pass, compute entropy loss, and update affine batch-norm parameters.

    Args:
        x (Tensor): Input batch tensor.
        model (nn.Module): The model configured for Tent adaptation.
        optimizer (Optimizer): Optimizer for updating γ, β of BatchNorm2d layers.

    Returns:
        Tensor: Raw model outputs (logits) before softmax.
    """
    outputs = model(x)
    loss = softmax_entropy(outputs).mean()
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()
    optimizer.zero_grad()
    return outputs


class Tent(nn.Module):
    """
    Tent: Test-Time Entropy Minimization adaptation module.

    Wraps a model and optimizer; on each forward, computes entropy loss
    and updates batch-norm affine parameters online.
    Supports episodic resets to original state if desired.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        model: nn.Module,
        optimizer: optim.Optimizer,
        steps: int = 1,
        episodic: bool = False
    ) -> None:
        """
        Initialize Tent adapter.

        Args:
            model (nn.Module): Model configured via `configure_model_for_bn_affine`.
            optimizer (Optimizer): Optimizer on batch-norm params.
            steps (int): Number of gradient steps per forward call (>=1).
            episodic (bool): If True, reset to initial state before each forward.
        """
        super().__init__()
        assert steps > 0, "Tent requires at least one adaptation step"
        self.model = model
        self.optimizer = optimizer
        self.steps = steps
        self.episodic = episodic
        # Save initial state for episodic resets
        self._init_model_state, self._init_optim_state = copy_model_and_optimizer(
            self.model, self.optimizer)

    def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
        """
        Forward input through the model and adapt in-place.

        Args:
            x (Tensor): Input batch tensor.

        Returns:
            Tensor: Model outputs after adaptation steps.
        """
        if self.episodic:
            self.reset()

        outputs: Optional[Tensor] = None
        for _ in range(self.steps):
            outputs = forward_and_adapt(x, self.model, self.optimizer)
        assert outputs is not None, "No forward pass was executed"
        return outputs

    def reset(self) -> None:
        """
        Reset model and optimizer to their initial states.
        """
        if self._init_model_state is None or self._init_optim_state is None:
            raise RuntimeError("Cannot reset Tent without initial saved states")
        load_model_and_optimizer(
            self.model, self.optimizer,
            self._init_model_state, self._init_optim_state
        )
code.test:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    torch.manual_seed(0)

    # Define a simple CNN with BatchNorm layers
    class SimpleCNN(nn.Module):
        def __init__(self, in_channels: int = 3, num_classes: int = 10):
            super().__init__()
            self.features = nn.Sequential(
                nn.Conv2d(in_channels, 16, 3, padding=1),
                nn.BatchNorm2d(16),
                nn.ReLU(),
                nn.Conv2d(16, 32, 3, padding=1),
                nn.BatchNorm2d(32),
                nn.ReLU(),
                nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1)),
            )
            self.classifier = nn.Linear(32, num_classes)

        def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
            x = self.features(x)
            x = x.view(x.size(0), -1)
            return self.classifier(x)

    # Instantiate and configure model
    model = SimpleCNN()
    model = configure_model_for_bn_affine(model)

    # Collect BatchNorm affine parameters
    params, names = collect_bn_affine_params(model)
    print("Adaptable parameters:", names)

    # Set up optimizer and Tent adapter
    optimizer = optim.Adam(params, lr=1e-3)
    tent_adapter = Tent(model, optimizer, steps=1, episodic=False)

    # Generate a random batch
    x = torch.randn(8, 3, 32, 32)

    # Compute initial entropy
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits_init = model(x)
        ent_init = entropy_minimization_loss(logits_init).item()
    print(f"Initial entropy: {ent_init:.4f}")

    # Forward & adapt
    logits_adapted = tent_adapter(x)
    ent_after = entropy_minimization_loss(logits_adapted).item()
    print(f"Entropy after adaptation: {ent_after:.4f}")
code.documentation:
This module implements the Tent (Test-time Entropy Minimization) adaptation algorithm. It includes:
1. `softmax_entropy` and `entropy_minimization_loss` to compute the entropy-based unsupervised loss.
2. Utilities `collect_bn_affine_params` and `configure_model_for_bn_affine` to isolate and enable gradients only for BatchNorm2d affine parameters.
3. State-saving functions `copy_model_and_optimizer` and `load_model_and_optimizer` for episodic resets.
4. The core `Tent` class that wraps a model and optimizer, performing one or more adaptation steps per forward pass by minimizing prediction entropy.
A TEST BLOCK demonstrates usage with a toy CNN: it configures the model, wraps it in `Tent`, and shows entropy reduction on random inputs after one adaptation step.

components.name: Entropy Minimization Objective
components.description: A test-time unsupervised loss defined as the Shannon entropy of the model's output distribution on a batch of target inputs.  For logits z = f_{θ}(x^t), obtain probabilities ŷ = softmax(z) and compute H(ŷ)=−∑_{c} ŷ_{c} log ŷ_{c}.  Minimizing this objective encourages confident predictions and exploits the empirical correlation between low entropy and low error under distribution shift.

code.implementation:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
from typing import Literal


def softmax_entropy(logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Compute the Shannon entropy for each sample in a batch from model logits.

    Given logits z of shape (batch_size, num_classes), this function
    applies softmax to obtain class probabilities and then computes
    entropy H(p) = -sum_c p_c * log(p_c) for each sample.

    Parameters:
        logits (torch.Tensor): Unnormalized model outputs of shape
            (batch_size, num_classes).

    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: A tensor of shape (batch_size,) containing the
            entropy of each sample's predicted distribution.
    """
    # Numerically stable log-softmax
    log_probs = F.log_softmax(logits, dim=1)
    probs = torch.exp(log_probs)
    entropy = -(probs * log_probs).sum(dim=1)
    return entropy


def entropy_minimization_loss(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    reduction: Literal['mean', 'sum', 'none'] = 'mean'
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Compute the entropy minimization loss over a batch of logits.

    This unsupervised loss is defined as the Shannon entropy of the
    model's output distribution. Minimizing this encourages the model
    to produce confident (low-entropy) predictions.

    Parameters:
        logits (torch.Tensor): Unnormalized model outputs of shape
            (batch_size, num_classes).
        reduction (str): Specifies the reduction to apply to the output:
            'mean' | 'sum' | 'none'. 'mean': average over batch,
            'sum': sum over batch, 'none': no reduction.

    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: A scalar loss if reduction is 'mean' or 'sum',
            else a tensor of shape (batch_size,).
    """
    ent = softmax_entropy(logits)
    if reduction == 'mean':
        return ent.mean()
    elif reduction == 'sum':
        return ent.sum()
    elif reduction == 'none':
        return ent
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid reduction mode: {reduction}")
code.test:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example usage with random logits
    batch_size = 8
    num_classes = 5
    torch.manual_seed(0)

    # Simulate model outputs (logits)
    dummy_logits = torch.randn(batch_size, num_classes)

    # Compute per-sample entropy
    ent_per_sample = softmax_entropy(dummy_logits)
    print("Per-sample entropy:", ent_per_sample)

    # Compute mean entropy loss
    loss_mean = entropy_minimization_loss(dummy_logits, reduction='mean')
    print("Entropy minimization loss (mean):", loss_mean.item())

    # Compute sum entropy loss
    loss_sum = entropy_minimization_loss(dummy_logits, reduction='sum')
    print("Entropy minimization loss (sum):", loss_sum.item())
code.documentation:
This module provides a core implementation of the entropy minimization objective
used for test-time adaptation under distribution shift. The function softmax_entropy
computes the Shannon entropy of the predicted softmax distribution for each sample.
The function entropy_minimization_loss aggregates these entropies into a scalar
loss via configurable reductions ('mean', 'sum', or 'none'). Users can plug in
model logits directly to obtain the unsupervised loss that encourages confident
predictions. This loss can be seamlessly integrated into existing PyTorch training
or adaptation loops without additional dependencies.

components.name: Feature Modulation via BatchNorm Affine Parameters
components.description:
A parameterization strategy in which only the channel-wise affine transformations of the model's normalization layers are adapted at test time.  For each normalization layer ℓ and channel k, Tent maintains:
 • Normalization statistics μ_{ℓ,k}, σ_{ℓ,k}, estimated on the current test batch in the forward pass.
 • Learnable scale γ_{ℓ,k} and shift β_{ℓ,k} parameters, initialized from the source model.
During adaptation, γ and β are the only trainable parameters; all other θ remains frozen.  This low-dimensional choice (<1% of total parameters) yields stable and efficient test-time updates.

code.implementation:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch import Tensor
from typing import List, Tuple


def collect_bn_affine_params(model: nn.Module) -> Tuple[List[nn.Parameter], List[str]]:
    """
    Collect the affine scale (weight) and shift (bias) parameters from all
    BatchNorm2d layers in the model.

    Only the 'weight' and 'bias' parameters of each BatchNorm2d module are returned.

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): The model containing BatchNorm2d layers.

    Returns:
        params (List[nn.Parameter]): List of parameters to be adapted.
        names (List[str]): List of parameter names corresponding to `params`.
    """
    params: List[nn.Parameter] = []
    names: List[str] = []
    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if isinstance(module, nn.BatchNorm2d):
            for param_name, param in module.named_parameters(recurse=False):
                if param_name in ("weight", "bias"):
                    params.append(param)
                    names.append(f"{module_name}.{param_name}")
    return params, names


def configure_model_for_bn_affine(model: nn.Module) -> nn.Module:
    """
    Configure a neural network model for test-time adaptation via
    BatchNorm affine parameters only.

    - Puts the model in train mode so that BatchNorm uses batch statistics.
    - Disables gradient for all parameters, then re-enables gradient only for
      BatchNorm2d weight and bias.
    - Forces BatchNorm2d layers to use batch statistics (no running average).

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): The pre-trained model to configure.

    Returns:
        model (nn.Module): The same model, configured for adaptation.
    """
    # Enable train mode to ensure BatchNorm uses batch statistics
    model.train()
    # Disable gradients for all parameters
    model.requires_grad_(False)

    # Configure each BatchNorm2d layer
    for module in model.modules():
        if isinstance(module, nn.BatchNorm2d):
            # Enable gradient only for affine parameters
            module.requires_grad_(True)
            # Disable running statistics: force per-batch normalization
            module.track_running_stats = False
            # Clear any existing running stats
            module.running_mean = None  # type: ignore
            module.running_var = None   # type: ignore

    return model
code.test:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example: simple CNN with BatchNorm2d layers
    class SimpleCNN(nn.Module):
        def __init__(self):
            super().__init__()
            self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 16, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
            self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(16)
            self.relu = nn.ReLU()
            self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(16, 32, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
            self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(32)
            self.flatten = nn.Flatten()
            self.fc = nn.Linear(32 * 32 * 32, 10)

        def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor:
            x = self.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))
            x = self.relu(self.bn2(self.conv2(x)))
            x = self.flatten(x)
            return self.fc(x)

    # Instantiate and configure the model
    model = SimpleCNN()
    configured_model = configure_model_for_bn_affine(model)

    # Collect Trainable BatchNorm affine parameters
    params, names = collect_bn_affine_params(configured_model)

    # Print out parameter names and check requires_grad
    print("Adaptable BatchNorm affine parameters:")
    for name, param in zip(names, params):
        print(f"  {name} | requires_grad={param.requires_grad}")

    # Ensure other parameters are frozen
    all_params = list(model.named_parameters())
    print("\nOther parameters (should be frozen):")
    for name, param in all_params:
        if name not in names:
            print(f"  {name} | requires_grad={param.requires_grad}")
code.documentation:
This module provides utilities to adapt a pre-trained model at test time by only updating the
affine (scale and shift) parameters of its BatchNorm2d layers, leaving all other weights frozen.
The function `configure_model_for_bn_affine` puts the model into train mode, disables gradients
globally, re-enables gradients for only BatchNorm2d weight and bias, and forces per-batch
normalization by disabling running statistics. The function `collect_bn_affine_params` walks
through the model to gather these affine parameters and their names for optimizer setup.
In practice, one would pass the returned parameters to an optimizer and perform entropy
minimization or another adaptation loss. The TEST BLOCK demonstrates usage on a simple CNN,
showing which parameters are trainable versus frozen.

components.name: Test-Time Adaptation Algorithm
components.description:
An iterative procedure that updates the affine feature modulation parameters on each incoming test batch:
1. Initialization: extract and freeze all model parameters except normalization-layer affine γ, β; discard source-computed running statistics.
2. Iteration (for each test batch X^t):
   a. Forward pass: for each normalization layer, compute batch statistics μ, σ on X^t and normalize features; apply current γ, β to transform features; compute model logits and probabilities ŷ.
   b. Loss computation: compute batch-entropy loss L_ent = H(ŷ).
   c. Backward pass: compute gradients ∇_{γ,β} L_ent and update γ, β via SGD/Adam with a small learning rate.
   d. (Optional offline mode) repeat forward with updated γ, β to produce final predictions.
3. Termination: in online mode, continue indefinitely as test data arrives; in offline mode, run for a fixed number of epochs over target data.

code.implementation:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from copy import deepcopy
from typing import List, Tuple, Optional


def softmax_entropy(logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Compute the entropy of the softmax distribution for each sample in the batch.

    Args:
        logits (torch.Tensor): Model outputs of shape (B, C, ...), where C is number of classes.

    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: Entropy for each sample, shape (B,).
    """
    p = logits.softmax(dim=1)
    return -(p * p.log()).sum(dim=1)


@torch.enable_grad()
def forward_and_adapt(
    x: torch.Tensor,
    model: nn.Module,
    optimizer: optim.Optimizer
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Perform a forward pass, compute entropy loss, and update affine batch-norm parameters.

    Args:
        x (torch.Tensor): Input batch tensor.
        model (nn.Module): The model configured for Tent adaptation.
        optimizer (optim.Optimizer): Optimizer for updating γ, β of batch-norm layers.

    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: Raw model outputs (logits) before softmax.
    """
    outputs = model(x)
    loss = softmax_entropy(outputs).mean()
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()
    optimizer.zero_grad()
    return outputs


def collect_params(model: nn.Module) -> List[nn.Parameter]:
    """
    Collect all batch-norm affine scale (weight) and shift (bias) parameters.

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): Model containing BatchNorm2d layers.

    Returns:
        List[nn.Parameter]: List of parameters to adapt.
    """
    params: List[nn.Parameter] = []
    for module in model.modules():
        if isinstance(module, nn.BatchNorm2d):
            # weight is γ (scale), bias is β (shift)
            if module.weight is not None:
                params.append(module.weight)
            if module.bias is not None:
                params.append(module.bias)
    return params


def configure_model(model: nn.Module) -> nn.Module:
    """
    Prepare the model for test-time adaptation:
    - Freeze all parameters.
    - Enable gradient only for batch-norm affine parameters.
    - Disable running statistics; use only per-batch stats.

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): Pre-trained model to adapt.

    Returns:
        nn.Module: Configured model ready for Tent adaptation.
    """
    model.train()
    model.requires_grad_(False)
    for module in model.modules():
        if isinstance(module, nn.BatchNorm2d):
            module.requires_grad_(True)
            module.track_running_stats = False
            module.running_mean = None  # discard source statistics
            module.running_var = None
    return model


def copy_model_and_optimizer(
    model: nn.Module,
    optimizer: optim.Optimizer
) -> Tuple[dict, dict]:
    """
    Deep-copy model and optimizer states for episodic reset.

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): The model to copy.
        optimizer (optim.Optimizer): The optimizer to copy.

    Returns:
        Tuple[dict, dict]: (model_state_dict, optimizer_state_dict)
    """
    model_state = deepcopy(model.state_dict())
    optim_state = deepcopy(optimizer.state_dict())
    return model_state, optim_state


def load_model_and_optimizer(
    model: nn.Module,
    optimizer: optim.Optimizer,
    model_state: dict,
    optim_state: dict
) -> None:
    """
    Restore model and optimizer states.

    Args:
        model (nn.Module): Model to restore.
        optimizer (optim.Optimizer): Optimizer to restore.
        model_state (dict): Model state_dict to load.
        optim_state (dict): Optimizer state_dict to load.
    """
    model.load_state_dict(model_state)
    optimizer.load_state_dict(optim_state)


class Tent(nn.Module):
    """
    Tent: Test-Time Entropy Minimization adaptation module.

    Wraps a model and optimizer; on each forward, computes entropy loss
    and updates batch-norm affine parameters online.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        model: nn.Module,
        optimizer: optim.Optimizer,
        steps: int = 1,
        episodic: bool = False
    ) -> None:
        """
        Initialize Tent adapter.

        Args:
            model (nn.Module): Model configured via configure_model().
            optimizer (optim.Optimizer): Optimizer on batch-norm params.
            steps (int): Number of gradient steps per forward call.
            episodic (bool): If True, reset to initial state before each forward.
        """
        super().__init__()
        assert steps > 0, "Tent requires at least one adaptation step"
        self.model = model
        self.optimizer = optimizer
        self.steps = steps
        self.episodic = episodic
        # Save initial state for episodic resets
        self._init_model_state, self._init_optim_state = copy_model_and_optimizer(model, optimizer)

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Forward input through the model and adapt in-place.

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor): Input batch tensor.

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor: Model outputs after adaptation steps.
        """
        if self.episodic:
            self.reset()
        outputs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
        for _ in range(self.steps):
            outputs = forward_and_adapt(x, self.model, self.optimizer)
        assert outputs is not None, "No forward pass was executed"
        return outputs

    def reset(self) -> None:
        """
        Reset model and optimizer to their initial states.
        """
        if self._init_model_state is None or self._init_optim_state is None:
            raise RuntimeError("Cannot reset Tent without initial saved states")
        load_model_and_optimizer(self.model, self.optimizer,
                                 self._init_model_state,
                                 self._init_optim_state)
code.test:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Create a toy model with BatchNorm2d layers
    class ToyNet(nn.Module):
        def __init__(self):
            super().__init__()
            self.features = nn.Sequential(
                nn.Conv2d(3, 16, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
                nn.BatchNorm2d(16),
                nn.ReLU(),
                nn.Conv2d(16, 10, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
                nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1),
                nn.Flatten()
            )

        def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
            return self.features(x)

    # Instantiate and configure
    base_model = ToyNet()
    adapt_model = configure_model(base_model)
    params_to_adapt = collect_params(adapt_model)
    optimizer = optim.Adam(params_to_adapt, lr=1e-3)
    tent_model = Tent(adapt_model, optimizer, steps=2, episodic=True)

    # Simulate a batch of test data
    x_test = torch.randn(8, 3, 32, 32)
    # Perform test-time adaptation and get outputs
    outputs = tent_model(x_test)
    print("Output shape:", outputs.shape)
    # Compute entropy before and after adaptation for inspection
    with torch.no_grad():
        base_out = base_model(x_test)
        initial_entropy = softmax_entropy(base_out).mean().item()
    adapted_entropy = softmax_entropy(outputs).mean().item()
    print(f"Entropy before adapt: {initial_entropy:.4f}, after adapt: {adapted_entropy:.4f}")
code.documentation: This module implements the Tent (Test-time Entropy Minimization) adaptation algorithm. It configures a pre-trained model by freezing all parameters except the affine weights on BatchNorm2d layers and disabling running statistics, so that only per-batch moments are used. During each forward pass, it computes the softmax entropy loss on a batch of test inputs, backpropagates gradients only through these affine parameters, and takes a configurable number of optimizer steps online. The Tent class supports episodic resets, restoring the model and optimizer to their initial state before each new batch when desired. Core functions include entropy computation, parameter collection, and state-saving utilities. A toy example at the end demonstrates how to wrap a simple CNN and observe decreased entropy after adaptation.
